tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, significant proteinuria, low urine specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance, suggesting impaired renal function, possibly chronic kidney disease. Urine ketones are mildly elevated. Blood glucose is normal, but HbA1c is unexpectedly low—this may warrant repeat testing or evaluation for anemia or hemoglobinopathies. No evidence of urinary tract infection. Recommend nephrology evaluation and further assessment of renal function and possible underlying causes.

display.value: Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
